key_insight: The paper proves that the long Barzilai–Borwein (BB1) method is not superlinear on a positive-measure open set of strictly convex quadratics for every dimension n≥4.
engineering_relevance: This challenges the idea that BB methods should be expected to accelerate automatically on all well-conditioned quadratic optimization problems, which matters for optimization solvers and tuning heuristics.
method: Constructs an explicit open family of quadratic objectives and initial points where BB1 converges but only at geometric rates.
Uses a computer-assisted proof of a nonresonant attracting seven-cycle in the projectivized BB dynamics in 4D.
Derives two-sided bounds for spectral gradient components, gradient norm, and energy norm; objective gap decays with squared rates.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports explicit constants (ρ_min=10^-6, ρ_max=0.61) and a positive-Lebesgue-measure counterexample family, with convergence characterized by geometric lower/upper bounds rather than superlinear decay.
watch_item: The result is a rigorous worst-case construction for BB1 on quadratics; it does not imply typical practical failures outside the specific open set or under BB variants and safeguards.
paper_title: Barzilai-Borwein Fails Superlinear Convergence on an Open Set of Quadratics for Every Dimension $n\geq 4$
